sections.paragraph:
an unused root-

(1) i.q. Arabic صَرُحَ II. to be clear, manifest, open; whence صُرْحَةُ lofty ground; صَرْحُ a high building, a tower, Hebr. צָרִיחַ. Kindred are צָחַר, צָהַר.

(2) It is applied to the voice (compare צָהַל), hence to cry out with a loud and clear voice. Arab. صَرَخَ Eth. ጠርአ፡ Zep 1:14. Kindred is רָזַח.

Hiphil, to lift up a cry, used of a warlike cry, Isa 42:13.
